message.content: For the login/register part of onboarding, can we make it more like "press enter to authenticate" than needing to type something out?

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- benchmark: Performance regression detection using the browse daemon. Establishes
baselines for page load times, Core Web Vitals, and resource sizes.
Compares before/after on every PR. Tracks performance trends over time.
Use when: "performance", "benchmark", "page speed", "lighthouse", "web vitals",
"bundle size", "load time". (gstack)
Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "speed test", "check performance".
- design-html: Design finalization: generates production-quality Pretext-native HTML/CSS.
Works with approved mockups from /design-shotgun, CEO plans from /plan-ceo-review,
design review context from /plan-design-review, or from scratch with a user
description. Text actually reflows, heights are computed, layouts are dynamic.
30KB overhead, zero deps. Smart API routing: picks the right Pretext patterns
for each design type. Use when: "finalize this design", "turn this into HTML",
"build me a page", "implement this design", or after any planning skill.
Proactively suggest when user has approved a design or has a plan ready. (gstack)
Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "build the design", "code the mockup", "make it real".
- design-shotgun: Design shotgun: generate multiple AI design variants, open a comparison board,
collect structured feedback, and iterate. Standalone design exploration you can
run anytime. Use when: "explore designs", "show me options", "design variants",
"visual brainstorm", or "I don't like how this looks".
Proactively suggest when the user describes a UI feature but hasn't seen
what it could look like. (gstack)
- plan-design-review: Designer's eye plan review — interactive, like CEO and Eng review.
Rates each design dimension 0-10, explains what would make it a 10,
then fixes the plan to get there. Works in plan mode. For live site
visual audits, use /design-review. Use when asked to "review the design plan"
or "design critique".
Proactively suggest when the user has a plan with UI/UX components that
should be reviewed before implementation. (gstack)
- autoplan: Auto-review pipeline — reads the full CEO, design, eng, and DX review skills from disk
and runs them sequentially with auto-decisions using 6 decision principles. Surfaces
taste decisions (close approaches, borderline scope, codex disagreements) at a final
approval gate. One command, fully reviewed plan out.
Use when asked to "auto review", "autoplan", "run all reviews", "review this plan
automatically", or "make the decisions for me".
Proactively suggest when the user has a plan file and wants to run the full review
gauntlet without answering 15-30 intermediate questions. (gstack)
Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "auto plan", "automatic review".
- design-consultation: Design consultation: understands your product, researches the landscape, proposes a
complete design system (aesthetic, typography, color, layout, spacing, motion), and
generates font+color preview pages. Creates DESIGN.md as your project's design source
of truth. For existing sites, use /plan-design-review to infer the system instead.
Use when asked to "design system", "brand guidelines", or "create DESIGN.md".
Proactively suggest when starting a new project's UI with no existing
design system or DESIGN.md. (gstack)
- learn: Manage project learnings. Review, search, prune, and export what gstack
has learned across sessions. Use when asked to "what have we learned",
"show learnings", "prune stale learnings", or "export learnings".
Proactively suggest when the user asks about past patterns or wonders
"didn't we fix this before?"
- freeze: Restrict file edits to a specific directory for the session. Blocks Edit and
Write outside the allowed path. Use when debugging to prevent accidentally
"fixing" unrelated code, or when you want to scope changes to one module.
Use when asked to "freeze", "restrict edits", "only edit this folder",
or "lock down edits". (gstack)
- checkpoint: Save and resume working state checkpoints. Captures git state, decisions made,
and remaining work so you can pick up exactly where you left off — even across
Conductor workspace handoffs between branches.
Use when asked to "checkpoint", "save progress", "where was I", "resume",
"what was I working on", or "pick up where I left off".
Proactively suggest when a session is ending, the user is switching context,
or before a long break. (gstack)
- careful: Safety guardrails for destructive commands. Warns before rm -rf, DROP TABLE,
force-push, git reset --hard, kubectl delete, and similar destructive operations.
User can override each warning. Use when touching prod, debugging live systems,
or working in a shared environment. Use when asked to "be careful", "safety mode",
"prod mode", or "careful mode". (gstack)
- cso: Chief Security Officer mode. Infrastructure-first security audit: secrets archaeology,
dependency supply chain, CI/CD pipeline security, LLM/AI security, skill supply chain
scanning, plus OWASP Top 10, STRIDE threat modeling, and active verification.
Two modes: daily (zero-noise, 8/10 confidence gate) and comprehensive (monthly deep
scan, 2/10 bar). Trend tracking across audit runs.
Use when: "security audit", "threat model", "pentest review", "OWASP", "CSO review". (gstack)
Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "see-so", "see so", "security review", "security check", "vulnerability scan", "run security".
- canary: Post-deploy canary monitoring. Watches the live app for console errors,
performance regressions, and page failures using the browse daemon. Takes
periodic screenshots, compares against pre-deploy baselines, and alerts
on anomalies. Use when: "monitor deploy", "canary", "post-deploy check",
"watch production", "verify deploy". (gstack)
- open-gstack-browser: Launch GStack Browser — AI-controlled Chromium with the sidebar extension baked in.
Opens a visible browser window where you can watch every action in real time.
The sidebar shows a live activity feed and chat. Anti-bot stealth built in.
Use when asked to "open gstack browser", "launch browser", "connect chrome",
"open chrome", "real browser", "launch chrome", "side panel", or "control my browser".
Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "show me the browser".
- investigate: Systematic debugging with root cause investigation. Four phases: investigate,
analyze, hypothesize, implement. Iron Law: no fixes without root cause.
Use when asked to "debug this", "fix this bug", "why is this broken",
"investigate this error", or "root cause analysis".
Proactively invoke this skill (do NOT debug directly) when the user reports
errors, 500 errors, stack traces, unexpected behavior, "it was working
yesterday", or is troubleshooting why something stopped working. (gstack)
- document-release: Post-ship documentation update. Reads all project docs, cross-references the
diff, updates README/ARCHITECTURE/CONTRIBUTING/CLAUDE.md to match what shipped,
polishes CHANGELOG voice, cleans up TODOS, and optionally bumps VERSION. Use when
asked to "update the docs", "sync documentation", or "post-ship docs".
Proactively suggest after a PR is merged or code is shipped. (gstack)
- health: Code quality dashboard. Wraps existing project tools (type checker, linter,
test runner, dead code detector, shell linter), computes a weighted composite
0-10 score, and tracks trends over time. Use when: "health check",
"code quality", "how healthy is the codebase", "run all checks",
"quality score". (gstack)
- gstack-upgrade: Upgrade gstack to the latest version. Detects global vs vendored install,
runs the upgrade, and shows what's new. Use when asked to "upgrade gstack",
"update gstack", or "get latest version".
Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "upgrade the tools", "update the tools", "gee stack upgrade", "g stack upgrade".
- land-and-deploy: Land and deploy workflow. Merges the PR, waits for CI and deploy,
verifies production health via canary checks. Takes over after /ship
creates the PR. Use when: "merge", "land", "deploy", "merge and verify",
"land it", "ship it to production". (gstack)
- codex: OpenAI Codex CLI wrapper — three modes. Code review: independent diff review via
codex review with pass/fail gate. Challenge: adversarial mode that tries to break
your code. Consult: ask codex anything with session continuity for follow-ups.
The "200 IQ autistic developer" second opinion. Use when asked to "codex review",
"codex challenge", "ask codex", "second opinion", or "consult codex". (gstack)
Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "code x", "code ex", "get another opinion".
- gstack: Fast headless browser for QA testing and site dogfooding. Navigate pages, interact with
elements, verify state, diff before/after, take annotated screenshots, test responsive
layouts, forms, uploads, dialogs, and capture bug evidence. Use when asked to open or
test a site, verify a deployment, dogfood a user flow, or file a bug with screenshots. (gstack)
- qa: Systematically QA test a web application and fix bugs found. Runs QA testing,
then iteratively fixes bugs in source code, committing each fix atomically and
re-verifying. Use when asked to "qa", "QA", "test this site", "find bugs",
"test and fix", or "fix what's broken".
Proactively suggest when the user says a feature is ready for testing
or asks "does this work?". Three tiers: Quick (critical/high only),
Standard (+ medium), Exhaustive (+ cosmetic). Produces before/after health scores,
fix evidence, and a ship-readiness summary. For report-only mode, use /qa-only. (gstack)
Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "quality check", "test the app", "run QA".
- qa-only: Report-only QA testing. Systematically tests a web application and produces a
structured report with health score, screenshots, and repro steps — but never
fixes anything. Use when asked to "just report bugs", "qa report only", or
"test but don't fix". For the full test-fix-verify loop, use /qa instead.
Proactively suggest when the user wants a bug report without any code changes. (gstack)
Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "bug report", "just check for bugs".
- setup-browser-cookies: Import cookies from your real Chromium browser into the headless browse session.
Opens an interactive picker UI where you select which cookie domains to import.
Use before QA testing authenticated pages. Use when asked to "import cookies",
"login to the site", or "authenticate the browser". (gstack)
- last30days: Deep research engine covering the last 30 days across 10+ sources - Reddit, X/Twitter, YouTube, TikTok, Instagram, Hacker News, Polymarket, Bluesky, Truth Social, web. AI synthesizes findings into grounded, cited reports. The most comprehensive recency research skill on ClawHub.
- review: Pre-landing PR review. Analyzes diff against the base branch for SQL safety, LLM trust
boundary violations, conditional side effects, and other structural issues. Use when
asked to "review this PR", "code review", "pre-landing review", or "check my diff".
Proactively suggest when the user is about to merge or land code changes. (gstack)
- plan-ceo-review: CEO/founder-mode plan review. Rethink the problem, find the 10-star product,
challenge premises, expand scope when it creates a better product. Four modes:
SCOPE EXPANSION (dream big), SELECTIVE EXPANSION (hold scope + cherry-pick
expansions), HOLD SCOPE (maximum rigor), SCOPE REDUCTION (strip to essentials).
Use when asked to "think bigger", "expand scope", "strategy review", "rethink this",
or "is this ambitious enough".
Proactively suggest when the user is questioning scope or ambition of a plan,
or when the plan feels like it could be thinking bigger. (gstack)
- office-hours: YC Office Hours — two modes. Startup mode: six forcing questions that expose
demand reality, status quo, desperate specificity, narrowest wedge, observation,
and future-fit. Builder mode: design thinking brainstorming for side projects,
hackathons, learning, and open source. Saves a design doc.
Use when asked to "brainstorm this", "I have an idea", "help me think through
this", "office hours", or "is this worth building".
Proactively invoke this skill (do NOT answer directly) when the user describes
a new product idea, asks whether something is worth building, wants to think
through design decisions for something that doesn't exist yet, or is exploring
a concept before any code is written.
Use before /plan-ceo-review or /plan-eng-review. (gstack)
- retro: Weekly engineering retrospective. Analyzes commit history, work patterns,
and code quality metrics with persistent history and trend tracking.
Team-aware: breaks down per-person contributions with praise and growth areas.
Use when asked to "weekly retro", "what did we ship", or "engineering retrospective".
Proactively suggest at the end of a work week or sprint. (gstack)
- devex-review: Live developer experience audit. Uses the browse tool to actually TEST the
developer experience: navigates docs, tries the getting started flow, times
TTHW, screenshots error messages, evaluates CLI help text. Produces a DX
scorecard with evidence. Compares against /plan-devex-review scores if they
exist (the boomerang: plan said 3 minutes, reality says 8). Use when asked to
"test the DX", "DX audit", "developer experience test", or "try the
onboarding". Proactively suggest after shipping a developer-facing feature. (gstack)
Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "dx audit", "test the developer experience", "try the onboarding", "developer experience test".
- plan-devex-review: Developer Experience plan review. Evaluates plans through Addy Osmani's DX
framework: zero friction, learn by doing, fight uncertainty. Rates 8 DX
dimensions 0-10 with a DX Scorecard. Use when asked to "DX review",
"developer experience audit", "devex review", or "API design review".
Proactively suggest when the user has a plan for developer-facing products
(APIs, CLIs, SDKs, libraries, platforms, docs). (gstack)
Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "dx review", "developer experience review", "devex review", "devex audit", "API design review", "onboarding review".
- browse: Fast headless browser for QA testing and site dogfooding. Navigate any URL, interact with
elements, verify page state, diff before/after actions, take annotated screenshots, check
responsive layouts, test forms and uploads, handle dialogs, and assert element states.
~100ms per command. Use when you need to test a feature, verify a deployment, dogfood a
user flow, or file a bug with evidence. Use when asked to "open in browser", "test the
site", "take a screenshot", or "dogfood this". (gstack)
- design-review: Designer's eye QA: finds visual inconsistency, spacing issues, hierarchy problems,
AI slop patterns, and slow interactions — then fixes them. Iteratively fixes issues
in source code, committing each fix atomically and re-verifying with before/after
screenshots. For plan-mode design review (before implementation), use /plan-design-review.
Use when asked to "audit the design", "visual QA", "check if it looks good", or "design polish".
Proactively suggest when the user mentions visual inconsistencies or
wants to polish the look of a live site. (gstack)
- ship: Ship workflow: detect + merge base branch, run tests, review diff, bump VERSION,
update CHANGELOG, commit, push, create PR. Use when asked to "ship", "deploy",
"push to main", "create a PR", "merge and push", or "get it deployed".
Proactively invoke this skill (do NOT push/PR directly) when the user says code
is ready, asks about deploying, wants to push code up, or asks to create a PR. (gstack)
- plan-eng-review: Eng manager-mode plan review. Lock in the execution plan — architecture,
data flow, diagrams, edge cases, test coverage, performance. Walks through
issues interactively with opinionated recommendations. Use when asked to
"review the architecture", "engineering review", or "lock in the plan".
Proactively suggest when the user has a plan or design doc and is about to
start coding — to catch architecture issues before implementation. (gstack)
Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "tech review", "technical review", "plan engineering review".
- guard: Full safety mode: destructive command warnings + directory-scoped edits.
Combines /careful (warns before rm -rf, DROP TABLE, force-push, etc.) with
/freeze (blocks edits outside a specified directory). Use for maximum safety
when touching prod or debugging live systems. Use when asked to "guard mode",
"full safety", "lock it down", or "maximum safety". (gstack)
- unfreeze: Clear the freeze boundary set by /freeze, allowing edits to all directories
again. Use when you want to widen edit scope without ending the session.
Use when asked to "unfreeze", "unlock edits", "remove freeze", or
"allow all edits". (gstack)
- setup-deploy: Configure deployment settings for /land-and-deploy. Detects your deploy
platform (Fly.io, Render, Vercel, Netlify, Heroku, GitHub Actions, custom),
production URL, health check endpoints, and deploy status commands. Writes
the configuration to CLAUDE.md so all future deploys are automatic.
Use when: "setup deploy", "configure deployment", "set up land-and-deploy",
"how do I deploy with gstack", "add deploy config".
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: Let me look at the current onboarding flow.

content.content:
1	"""Octopus CLI — command-line interface for workspaces, notebooks, tables, history, and search."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import json
6	import sys
7	from pathlib import Path
8	from typing import Optional
9	
10	import questionary
11	import typer
12	
13	from .client import OctopusClient, OctopusError
14	from .config import load_config, save_config
15	from .formatting import console, output_json, print_members, print_rooms, print_user
16	
17	app = typer.Typer(name="octopus", help="Octopus CLI — workspaces, notebooks, tables, history.")
18	
19	
20	def _client() -> OctopusClient:
21	    cfg = load_config()
22	    return OctopusClient(base_url=cfg["base_url"], api_key=cfg.get("api_key", ""))
23	
24	
25	def _use_json(flag: bool) -> bool:
26	    return flag or load_config().get("output_format") == "json"
27	
28	
29	def _default_workspace() -> str:
30	    ws = load_config().get("default_workspace", "")
31	    if not ws:
32	        console.print("[red]No default workspace. Run [bold]octopus connect[/bold] or set manually: octopus config default_workspace <id>[/red]")
33	        raise typer.Exit(1)
34	    return ws
35	
36	
37	def _err(e: OctopusError) -> None:
38	    console.print(f"[red]Error [{e.status_code}]: {e.detail}[/red]")
39	    raise typer.Exit(1)
40	
41	
42	# ===========================================================================
43	# Auth
44	# ===========================================================================
45	
46	@app.command()
47	def register(
48	    name: str = typer.Argument(...),
49	    password: str = typer.Option(None, "--password", help="Password for the account"),
50	    as_json: bool = typer.Option(False, "--json"),
51	):
52	    """Create account and store API key."""
53	    if not password:
54	        password = typer.prompt("Password", hide_input=True, confirmation_prompt=True)
55	    with _client() as c:
56	        try:
57	            data = c.register(name, description="", password=password)
58	        except OctopusError as e:
59	            _err(e)
60	    save_config(api_key=data["api_key"], username=data["name"])
61	    if _use_json(as_json):
62	        output_json(data)
63	    else:
64	        console.print(f"[green]Registered as {data['name']}[/green]  API key: [bold]{data['api_key']}[/bold]")
65	
66	
67	@app.command()
68	def login(name: str = typer.Argument(...), password: str = typer.Option(..., prompt=True, hide_input=True), as_json: bool = typer.Option(False, "--json")):
69	    """Login with password."""
70	    with _client() as c:
71	        try:
72	            data = c.login(name, password)
73	        except OctopusError as e:
74	            _err(e)
75	    save_config(api_key=data["api_key"], username=data["name"])
76	    if _use_json(as_json):
77	        output_json(data)
78	    else:
79	        console.print(f"[green]Logged in as {data['name']}[/green]")
80	
81	
82	@app.command()
83	def auth(base_url: str = typer.Argument(...), api_key: str = typer.Option(..., "--api-key")):
84	    """Store existing credentials."""
85	    save_config(base_url=base_url, api_key=api_key)
86	    with OctopusClient(base_url=base_url, api_key=api_key) as c:
87	        try:
88	            user = c.whoami()
89	            save_config(username=user["name"])
90	            console.print(f"[green]Authenticated as {user['name']}[/green]")
91	        except OctopusError:
92	            console.print("[yellow]Saved but could not verify.[/yellow]")
93	
94	
95	@app.command()
96	def whoami(as_json: bool = typer.Option(False, "--json")):
97	    """Show profile."""
98	    with _client() as c:
99	        try:
100	            data = c.whoami()
101	        except OctopusError as e:
102	            _err(e)
103	    if _use_json(as_json):
104	        output_json(data)
105	    else:
106	        print_user(data)
107	
108	
109	# ===========================================================================
110	# Workspaces
111	# ===========================================================================
112	
113	ws_app = typer.Typer(help="Workspace management.")
114	app.add_typer(ws_app, name="workspaces")
115	
116	
117	@ws_app.command("list")
118	def ws_list(mine: bool = typer.Option(False, "--mine"), as_json: bool = typer.Option(False, "--json")):
119	    """List workspaces."""
120	    with _client() as c:
121	        try:
122	            data = c.list_workspaces(mine=mine)
123	        except OctopusError as e:
124	            _err(e)
125	    if _use_json(as_json):
126	        output_json(data)
127	    else:
128	        print_rooms(data, title="My Workspaces" if mine else "Public Workspaces")
129	
130	
131	@ws_app.command("create")
132	def ws_create(name: str = typer.Argument(...), description: str = typer.Option(""), public: bool = typer.Option(False, "--public"), as_json: bool = typer.Option(False, "--json")):
133	    """Create workspace."""
134	    with _client() as c:
135	        try:
136	            data = c.create_workspace(name, description=description, is_public=public)
137	        except OctopusError as e:
138	            _err(e)
139	    if _use_json(as_json):
140	        output_json(data)
141	    else:
142	        console.print(f"[green]Created '{data['name']}'[/green]  ID: {data['id']}  Invite: {data['invite_code']}")
143	
144	
145	@ws_app.command("join")
146	def ws_join(invite_code: str = typer.Argument(...), as_json: bool = typer.Option(False, "--json")):
147	    """Join workspace by invite code."""
148	    with _client() as c:
149	        try:
150	            data = c.join_workspace(invite_code)
151	        except OctopusError as e:
152	            _err(e)
153	    if _use_json(as_json):
154	        output_json(data)
155	    else:
156	        console.print(f"[green]Joined '{data.get('name')}'[/green]")
157	
158	
159	@ws_app.command("info")
160	def ws_info(workspace_id: str = typer.Argument(...), as_json: bool = typer.Option(False, "--json")):
161	    """Show workspace details."""
162	    with _client() as c:
163	        try:
164	            data = c.get_workspace(workspace_id)
165	        except OctopusError as e:
166	            _err(e)
167	    if _use_json(as_json):
168	        output_json(data)
169	    else:
170	        console.print(f"[bold]{data['name']}[/bold]  Members: {data.get('member_count', '?')}  Public: {data['is_public']}")
171	        console.print(f"ID: {data['id']}  Invite: {data['invite_code']}")
172	
173	
174	@ws_app.command("members")
175	def ws_members(workspace_id: str = typer.Argument(...), as_json: bool = typer.Option(False, "--json")):
176	    """List workspace members."""
177	    with _client() as c:
178	        try:
179	            data = c.workspace_members(workspace_id)
180	        except OctopusError as e:
181	            _err(e)
182	    if _use_json(as_json):
183	        output_json(data)
184	    else:
185	        print_members(data)
186	
187	
188	# ===========================================================================
189	# Notebooks
190	# ===========================================================================
191	
192	nb_app = typer.Typer(help="Notebook collections (folders + markdown pages).")
193	app.add_typer(nb_app, name="notebooks")
194	
195	
196	@nb_app.command("list")
197	def nb_list(workspace_id: str = typer.Option(None, "--ws"), all_: bool = typer.Option(False, "--all"), as_json: bool = typer.Option(False, "--json")):
198	    """List notebooks. --all for cross-workspace, --ws for single workspace."""
199	    with _client() as c:
200	        try:
201	            data = c.all_notebooks() if all_ else c.list_notebooks(workspace_id or _default_workspace())
202	        except OctopusError as e:
203	            _err(e)
204	    if _use_json(as_json):

392	def _resolve_col_names(table: dict, data: dict) -> dict:
393	    """Translate column names to IDs in a data dict."""
394	    cols = table.get("columns", [])
395	    name_to_id = {col["name"]: col["id"] for col in cols}
396	    id_set = {col["id"] for col in cols}
397	    resolved = {}
398	    for k, v in data.items():
399	        if k in id_set:
400	            resolved[k] = v
401	        elif k in name_to_id:
402	            resolved[name_to_id[k]] = v
403	    return resolved
404	
405	
406	def _resolve_filter_names(table: dict, filters_json: str) -> str:
407	    """Resolve column names in filter JSON to column IDs."""
408	    if not filters_json:
409	        return filters_json
410	    cols = table.get("columns", [])
411	    name_to_id = {col["name"]: col["id"] for col in cols}
412	    parsed = json.loads(filters_json)
413	    for f in parsed:
414	        cid = f.get("column_id", "")
415	        if cid in name_to_id:
416	            f["column_id"] = name_to_id[cid]
417	    return json.dumps(parsed)
418	
419	
420	def _resolve_sort_name(table: dict, sort_by: str) -> str:
421	    """Resolve column name to ID for sorting."""
422	    if not sort_by:
423	        return sort_by
424	    cols = table.get("columns", [])
425	    name_to_id = {col["name"]: col["id"] for col in cols}
426	    return name_to_id.get(sort_by, sort_by)
427

592	@tables_app.command("import")
593	def tables_import(
594	    table_id: str = typer.Argument(...),
595	    file: str = typer.Option(None, "--file", "-f", help="CSV or JSON file path (or pipe via stdin)"),
596	    format_: str = typer.Option("auto", "--format", help="csv, json, or auto (detect from extension/content)"),
597	    workspace_id: str = typer.Option(None, "--ws"),
598	    as_json: bool = typer.Option(False, "--json"),
599	):
600	    """Bulk import rows from CSV or JSON. Auto-chunks into batches of 5000.
601	    CSV: first row is column headers. JSON: array of objects.
602	    Pipe: cat data.csv | octopus tables import <table_id> --format csv"""
603	    import csv as csv_mod
604	    import io as io_mod
605	
606	    # Read input
607	    if file:
608	        with open(file) as f:
609	            raw = f.read()
610	        if format_ == "auto":
611	            format_ = "csv" if file.endswith(".csv") else "json"
612	    elif not sys.stdin.isatty():
613	        raw = sys.stdin.read()
614	        if format_ == "auto":
615	            raw_stripped = raw.strip()
616	            format_ = "json" if raw_stripped.startswith("[") or raw_stripped.startswith("{") else "csv"
617	    else:
618	        console.print("[red]Provide --file or pipe data via stdin.[/red]")
619	        raise typer.Exit(1)
620	
621	    # Parse rows
622	    rows_data: list[dict] = []
623	    if format_ == "csv":
624	        reader = csv_mod.DictReader(io_mod.StringIO(raw))
625	        for row in reader:
626	            rows_data.append(dict(row))
627	    else:
628	        parsed = json.loads(raw)
629	        rows_data = parsed if isinstance(parsed, list) else [parsed]
630	
631	    if not rows_data:
632	        console.print("[dim]No rows to import.[/dim]")
633	        return
634	
635	    with _client() as c:
636	        try:
637	            ws = workspace_id or _default_workspace()
638	            table = c.get_table(ws, table_id)
639	
640	            # Resolve column names to IDs
641	            resolved_rows = [_resolve_col_names(table, r) for r in rows_data]
642	
643	            # Chunk into batches of 5000
644	            batch_size = 5000
645	            total_inserted = 0
646	            for i in range(0, len(resolved_rows), batch_size):
647	                batch = resolved_rows[i:i + batch_size]
648	                c.insert_table_rows_batch(ws, table_id, batch)
649	                total_inserted += len(batch)
650	                if len(resolved_rows) > batch_size:
651	                    console.print(f"  [dim]Inserted {total_inserted}/{len(resolved_rows)} rows...[/dim]")
652	        except OctopusError as e:
653	            _err(e)
654	
655	    if _use_json(as_json):
656	        output_json({"imported": total_inserted})
657	    else:
658	        console.print(f"[green]Imported {total_inserted} rows.[/green]")
659

content.text:
Now the auth step in `connect` uses:

1. **Login/Register** — a `questionary.select` with arrow keys, so you just press Enter on the default (Login)
2. **Username** — a `questionary.text` pre-filled with your system username (from `getpass.getuser()`), so you can just press Enter to accept it
3. **Password** — still uses `typer.prompt` with `hide_input=True` since you have to type that regardless

The flow goes from three typed-out prompts to mostly just pressing Enter through defaults.

content: You wanted the onboarding auth step to feel more like "press Enter to authenticate." I replaced the typed login/register prompt and username prompt with questionary selects and a pre-filled default username. The change is done but uncommitted. (disable recaps in /config)

from uuid import UUID

from ..auth import generate_api_key, hash_api_key, hash_password, verify_password
from ..database import get_pool


async def register_user(
    name: str,
    display_name: str | None,
    description: str = "",
    password: str | None = None,
) -> tuple[dict, str]:
    """Register a new user. Returns (user_row, raw_api_key)."""
    pool = get_pool()
    api_key = generate_api_key()
    key_hash = hash_api_key(api_key)
    pw_hash = hash_password(password) if password else None
    try:
        row = await pool.fetchrow(
            "INSERT INTO users (name, display_name, api_key_hash, password_hash, description) "
            "VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5) "
            "RETURNING id, name, display_name, description, created_at, last_seen",
            name,
            display_name or name,
            key_hash,
            pw_hash,
            description,
        )
    except Exception as e:
        if "unique" in str(e).lower() and "name" in str(e).lower():
            raise ValueError(f"Username '{name}' is already taken")
        raise
    user = dict(row)

    # Auto-provision a default workspace for new users.
    # workspaces.name is VARCHAR(128); trim the display so the suffix always fits.
    from . import workspace_service
    suffix = "'s Workspace"
    ws_name = f"{user['display_name'][: 128 - len(suffix)]}{suffix}"
    await workspace_service.create_workspace(
        name=ws_name,
        description="",
        creator_id=user["id"],
        is_public=False,
    )
    return user, api_key


async def get_user_by_id(user_id: UUID) -> dict | None:
    pool = get_pool()
    row = await pool.fetchrow(
        "SELECT id, name, display_name, description, created_at, last_seen "
        "FROM users WHERE id = $1",
        user_id,
    )
    return dict(row) if row else None


async def update_user(
    user_id: UUID,
    display_name: str | None = None,
    description: str | None = None,
    password: str | None = None,
) -> dict:
    pool = get_pool()
    sets = []
    args = []
    idx = 1
    if display_name is not None:
        sets.append(f"display_name = ${idx}")
        args.append(display_name)
        idx += 1
    if description is not None:
        sets.append(f"description = ${idx}")
        args.append(description)
        idx += 1
    if password is not None:
        sets.append(f"password_hash = ${idx}")
        args.append(hash_password(password))
        idx += 1
    if not sets:
        row = await pool.fetchrow(
            "SELECT id, name, display_name, description, created_at, last_seen "
            "FROM users WHERE id = $1",
            user_id,
        )
        return dict(row)
    args.append(user_id)
    row = await pool.fetchrow(
        f"UPDATE users SET {', '.join(sets)} WHERE id = ${idx} "
        "RETURNING id, name, display_name, description, created_at, last_seen",
        *args,
    )
    return dict(row)


async def authenticate_by_password(name: str, password: str) -> tuple[dict, str]:
    """Authenticate by username + password. Returns (user_dict, new_api_key)."""
    pool = get_pool()
    row = await pool.fetchrow(
        "SELECT id, name, display_name, description, created_at, last_seen, password_hash "
        "FROM users WHERE name = $1",
        name,
    )
    if not row or not row["password_hash"]:
        raise ValueError("Invalid username or password")
    if not verify_password(password, row["password_hash"]):
        raise ValueError("Invalid username or password")
    # Generate new API key on login
    api_key = generate_api_key()
    key_hash = hash_api_key(api_key)
    await pool.execute(
        "UPDATE users SET api_key_hash = $1, last_seen = now() WHERE id = $2",
        key_hash,
        row["id"],
    )
    user = {k: v for k, v in dict(row).items() if k != "password_hash"}
    return user, api_key

1	"""Synchronous httpx client wrapping the Octopus REST API."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import httpx
6	
7	
8	class OctopusError(Exception):
9	    def __init__(self, status_code: int, detail: str):
10	        self.status_code = status_code
11	        self.detail = detail
12	        super().__init__(f"[{status_code}] {detail}")
13	
14	
15	class OctopusClient:
16	    def __init__(self, base_url: str, api_key: str = ""):
17	        self._base_url = base_url.rstrip("/")
18	        self._api_key = api_key
19	        self._http = httpx.Client(base_url=self._base_url, timeout=30)
20	
21	    def close(self) -> None:
22	        self._http.close()
23	
24	    def __enter__(self):
25	        return self
26	
27	    def __exit__(self, *args):
28	        self.close()
29	
30	    def _headers(self) -> dict[str, str]:
31	        if not self._api_key:
32	            return {}
33	        return {"Authorization": f"Bearer {self._api_key}"}
34	
35	    def _request(self, method: str, path: str, **kwargs) -> httpx.Response:
36	        headers = kwargs.pop("headers", {})
37	        headers.update(self._headers())
38	        resp = self._http.request(method, path, headers=headers, **kwargs)
39	        if not resp.is_success:
40	            detail = ""
41	            try:
42	                detail = resp.json().get("detail", resp.text)
43	            except Exception:
44	                detail = resp.text
45	            raise OctopusError(resp.status_code, detail)
46	        return resp
47	
48	    def _get(self, path: str, **params) -> dict | list:
49	        return self._request("GET", path, params=params).json()

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive report.

## Web Frontend Auth/Login/Signin Pages - Findings

I've thoroughly explored the octopus project. Here's what I found:

### **Frontend Framework & Structure**
- **Framework**: Next.js 16.1.6 with React 19.2.3 (App Router pattern)
- **Location**: `/Users/henrydowling/projects/octopus/frontend/`
- **Language**: TypeScript with Tailwind CSS for styling

### **Login/Auth Pages**

1. **Login Page**: `/frontend/src/app/login/page.tsx`
   - Path: `/login` (accessible at frontend URL)
   - Provides username/password sign-in form
   - Calls backend endpoint `POST /api/v1/users/login`
   - Stores API key in localStorage under `octopus_token` key
   - Redirects authenticated users to their workspace or home page

2. **Homepage/Landing**: `/frontend/src/app/page.tsx`
   - Shows landing page with "Get Started" button linking to `/login`
   - Has logout/profile management in Header component

3. **Workspace Join Page**: `/frontend/src/app/join/[code]/page.tsx`
   - Path: `/join/[code]`
   - Allows users to join workspaces via invite codes
   - Requires user to be logged in first (redirects to `/login` if needed)

### **Authentication Flow**

**Frontend Auth Hook** (`/frontend/src/hooks/useAuth.ts`):
- Reads API key from localStorage
- Calls `GET /api/v1/users/me` to validate and load user profile
- Provides `user`, `loading`, `logout`, and `refresh` state

**API Functions** (`/frontend/src/lib/api.ts`):
- `register()` - `POST /api/v1/users/register`
- `loginWithPassword()` - `POST /api/v1/users/login`
- Token stored in localStorage as `octopus_token`
- Bearer token authentication in all API requests

### **Backend Auth Endpoints**

Located in `/backend/routers/users.py`:
- `POST /api/v1/users/register` - Create account with password
- `POST /api/v1/users/login` - Login with username/password
- `GET /api/v1/users/me` - Get current user profile
- `PATCH /api/v1/users/me` - Update profile
- `GET /api/v1/users/search` - Search users

**Auth Mechanism** (`/backend/auth.py`):
- API keys prefixed with `mc_` (generated via `secrets.token_urlsafe(32)`)
- Keys hashed with SHA-256 for database storage
- Passwords hashed with bcrypt
- FastAPI HTTPBearer security dependency

### **CLI Auth Commands**

Located in `/cli/main.py` (lines 42-93):
- `octopus register <name>` - Register with password
- `octopus login <name>` - Login with password
- `octopus auth <base_url> --api-key <key>` - Store existing credentials
- `octopus whoami` - Show current user profile

**NO device auth, OAuth, or browser-based CLI auth flows currently exist** - everything is password-based or manual API key entry.

### **UI Components**

Header component (`/frontend/src/components/Header.tsx`):
- Displays login/logout button
- Shows user profile dropdown with edit capability
- "Register / Login" link for unauthenticated users

### **Summary**
The project uses a **simple password-based authentication** system with API key generation. There are **no browser-opening flows, OAuth, or device authentication flows** currently implemented. Authentication is purely username/password based, either through the web login page or CLI prompts.

1	import hashlib
2	import secrets
3	import time
4	
5	import bcrypt
6	from fastapi import Depends, HTTPException, status
7	from fastapi.security import HTTPAuthorizationCredentials, HTTPBearer
8	
9	from .database import get_pool
10	
11	security = HTTPBearer()
12	
13	_LAST_SEEN_DEBOUNCE_SECONDS = 60
14	_LAST_SEEN_CACHE_SIZE = 4096
15	
16	
17	class _LastSeenCache:
18	    """Bounded LRU cache mapping user_id → monotonic timestamp of last DB write."""
19	
20	    def __init__(self, maxsize: int) -> None:
21	        self._data: dict[str, float] = {}
22	        self._maxsize = maxsize
23	
24	    def get(self, key: str) -> float:
25	        val = self._data.pop(key, 0.0)
26	        if val:
27	            self._data[key] = val
28	        return val
29	
30	    def set(self, key: str, value: float) -> None:
31	        self._data.pop(key, None)
32	        self._data[key] = value
33	        if len(self._data) > self._maxsize:
34	            oldest = next(iter(self._data))
35	            del self._data[oldest]
36	
37	
38	_last_seen_written = _LastSeenCache(_LAST_SEEN_CACHE_SIZE)
39	
40	
41	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
42	# API key helpers
43	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
44	
45	def generate_api_key() -> str:
46	    return "mc_" + secrets.token_urlsafe(32)
47	
48	
49	def hash_api_key(key: str) -> str:
50	    return hashlib.sha256(key.encode()).hexdigest()
51	
52	
53	def hash_password(password: str) -> str:
54	    return bcrypt.hashpw(password.encode(), bcrypt.gensalt()).decode()
55	
56	
57	def verify_password(password: str, password_hash: str) -> bool:
58	    return bcrypt.checkpw(password.encode(), password_hash.encode())
59	
60	
61	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
62	# FastAPI dependencies
63	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
64	
65	async def get_current_user(
66	    credentials: HTTPAuthorizationCredentials = Depends(security),
67	) -> dict:
68	    token: str = credentials.credentials
69	
70	    if not token.startswith("mc_"):
71	        raise HTTPException(
72	            status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED, detail="Invalid API key"
73	        )
74	
75	    key_hash = hash_api_key(token)
76	    pool = get_pool()
77	    row = await pool.fetchrow(
78	        "SELECT id, name, display_name, description, "
79	        "       created_at, last_seen "
80	        "FROM users WHERE api_key_hash = $1",
81	        key_hash,
82	    )
83	    if not row:
84	        raise HTTPException(
85	            status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED, detail="Invalid API key"
86	        )
87	    user = dict(row)
88

file.content:
import logging
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager

from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
from fastapi.requests import Request
from slowapi.errors import RateLimitExceeded
from slowapi.middleware import SlowAPIMiddleware
from starlette.responses import JSONResponse

from .config import settings
from .database import close_db, init_db
from .middleware import limiter
from .routers import (
    aggregate, files, memory, notebooks, skill, tables, users,
    workspaces,
)

logger = logging.getLogger("octopus")


@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    await init_db()
    yield
    await close_db()


def _rate_limit_exceeded_handler(request: Request, exc: RateLimitExceeded) -> JSONResponse:
    return JSONResponse(
        status_code=429,
        content={"detail": f"Rate limit exceeded: {exc.detail}"},
    )


app = FastAPI(
    title="Octopus",
    description="Shared memory for AI agents",
    version="0.1.0",
    lifespan=lifespan,
)

app.state.limiter = limiter
app.add_exception_handler(RateLimitExceeded, _rate_limit_exceeded_handler)
app.add_middleware(SlowAPIMiddleware)
app.add_middleware(CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=settings.CORS_ORIGINS,
    allow_credentials=True,
    allow_methods=["*"],
    allow_headers=["*"],
)
app.include_router(users.router)
app.include_router(workspaces.router)
app.include_router(notebooks.ws_router)
app.include_router(notebooks.personal_router)
app.include_router(memory.ws_router)
app.include_router(memory.personal_router)
app.include_router(tables.ws_router)
app.include_router(tables.personal_router)
app.include_router(files.ws_router)
app.include_router(files.personal_router)
app.include_router(aggregate.router)
app.include_router(skill.router)


@app.get("/health")
async def health():
    return {"status": "ok"}

@router.get("/search", response_model=list[UserSearchResult])
async def search_users(
    q: str = Query(..., min_length=1, max_length=64),
    current_user: dict = Depends(get_current_user),
):
    """Search for users by name or display name."""
    from ..database import get_pool
    pool = get_pool()
    rows = await pool.fetch(
        "SELECT id, name, display_name FROM users "
        "WHERE (name ILIKE $1 OR display_name ILIKE $1) AND id != $2 "
        "LIMIT 20",
        f"%{q}%",
        current_user["id"],
    )
    return [UserSearchResult(**dict(r)) for r in rows]

input.new_string:
# --- Step 2: Auth (browser-based) ---
    has_key = bool(cfg.get("api_key"))
    if has_key:
        try:
            with OctopusClient(base_url=base_url, api_key=cfg["api_key"]) as c:
                user = c.whoami()
            console.print(f"  [green]✓[/green] Already authenticated as [bold]{user['name']}[/bold]")
        except OctopusError:
            has_key = False

    if not has_key:
        import time as _time
        import webbrowser

        import httpx

        # Create a CLI auth session
        try:
            resp = httpx.post(f"{base_url}/api/v1/users/cli-auth/sessions", timeout=10)
            resp.raise_for_status()
            session_id = resp.json()["session_id"]
        except Exception as e:
            console.print(f"[red]Could not reach {base_url}: {e}[/red]")
            raise typer.Exit(1)

        # Build the login URL — for self-hosted, frontend is on port 3000
        if "localhost" in base_url or "127.0.0.1" in base_url:
            frontend_url = base_url.replace(":3456", ":3000")
        else:
            frontend_url = base_url
        login_url = f"{frontend_url}/login?cli={session_id}"

        console.print(f"\n  Opening browser to sign in...")
        console.print(f"  [dim]{login_url}[/dim]\n")
        webbrowser.open(login_url)

        _reserve_bottom_padding(4)
        console.print("  Waiting for authentication... [dim](press Ctrl+C to cancel)[/dim]")

        # Poll for the result
        try:
            for _ in range(120):  # 2 minutes max
                _time.sleep(1)
                try:
                    poll = httpx.get(f"{base_url}/api/v1/users/cli-auth/sessions/{session_id}", timeout=5)
                    if poll.status_code == 200:
                        result = poll.json()
                        if result["status"] == "complete":
                            save_config(api_key=result["api_key"], username=result["username"])
                            console.print(f"  [green]✓[/green] Logged in as [bold]{result['username']}[/bold]")
                            break
                except httpx.HTTPError:
                    pass
            else:
                console.print("[red]Timed out waiting for authentication.[/red]")
                raise typer.Exit(1)
        except KeyboardInterrupt:
            console.print("\n[yellow]Cancelled.[/yellow]")
            raise typer.Exit(1)

        _reserve_bottom_padding(4)
        name = questionary.text("Username", default=default_user).ask()
        if name is None:
            raise typer.Exit(1)
        name = name.strip()

        if action == "register":
            password = typer.prompt("Password", hide_input=True, confirmation_prompt=True)
            with OctopusClient(base_url=base_url, api_key="") as c:
                try:
                    data = c.register(name, description="", password=password)
                except OctopusError as e:
                    console.print(f"[red]Registration failed: {e.detail}[/red]")
                    raise typer.Exit(1)
            save_config(api_key=data["api_key"], username=data["name"])
            console.print(f"  [green]✓[/green] Registered as [bold]{data['name']}[/bold]")
        else:
            password = typer.prompt("Password", hide_input=True)
            with OctopusClient(base_url=base_url, api_key="") as c:
                try:
                    data = c.login(name, password)
                except OctopusError as e:
                    console.print(f"[red]Login failed: {e.detail}[/red]")
                    raise typer.Exit(1)
            save_config(api_key=data["api_key"], username=data["name"])
            console.print(f"  [green]✓[/green] Logged in as [bold]{data['name']}[/bold]")

content.text:
Both files parse cleanly. Here's a summary of the changes:

**Backend** (`backend/routers/users.py`): Added three new endpoints for CLI browser auth:
- `POST /api/v1/users/cli-auth/sessions` — creates a session, returns a `session_id`
- `GET /api/v1/users/cli-auth/sessions/{id}` — CLI polls this; returns `pending` or `complete` with `api_key`/`username`
- `POST /api/v1/users/cli-auth/sessions/{id}/approve` — frontend calls this after login to pass the API key back

Sessions are in-memory with a 10-minute TTL and automatic pruning.

**Frontend** (`frontend/src/app/login/page.tsx`):
- Detects `?cli=xxx` query param
- If already logged in, auto-approves the CLI session immediately
- After login/register, approves the CLI session and shows a "you can close this tab" confirmation
- Added sign-in/sign-up toggle so new users can register from the same page

**CLI** (`cli/main.py`): The `connect` auth step now:
1. Creates a CLI auth session via the API
2. Opens the browser to `/login?cli={session_id}`
3. Polls every second until the user authenticates (2-minute timeout)
4. No more username/password/login-vs-register prompts in the terminal

@app.command("connect")
def connect():
    """Interactive first-time setup. Sets base URL, authenticates, and configures defaults."""
    console.print("\n[bold]Octopus connect[/bold]  (press Enter to accept defaults)\n")

    # --- Step 0: Scope ---
    scope_options = [
        ("Everywhere on this machine (default)", "~/.octopus/config.json", "user"),
        ("Only this directory", "./.octopus/config.json", "project"),
    ]
    label_width = max(len(label) for label, _, _ in scope_options)
    _reserve_bottom_padding(8)
    scope = questionary.select(
        "Where do you want to install octopus?",
        choices=[
            questionary.Choice(f"{label:<{label_width}}   {path}", value=value)
            for label, path, value in scope_options
        ],
        use_shortcuts=True,
    ).ask()
    if scope is None:
        raise typer.Exit(1)

    # --- Step 1: API endpoint ---
    cfg = load_config()
    mode_options = [
        ("Managed", "hosted at getoctopus.com", "managed"),
        ("Self-host", "run on your own machine", "self"),
    ]
    mode_label_w = max(len(label) for label, _, _ in mode_options)
    _reserve_bottom_padding(8)
    mode = questionary.select(
        "How do you want to use Octopus?",
        choices=[
            questionary.Choice(f"{label:<{mode_label_w}}   ({desc})", value=value)
            for label, desc, value in mode_options
        ],
        use_shortcuts=True,
    ).ask()
    if mode is None:
        raise typer.Exit(1)

    if mode == "managed":
        base_url = "https://getoctopus.com"
    else:
        base_url = _self_host_walkthrough(cfg)
    save_config(base_url=base_url, scope=scope)

    # --- Step 2: Auth (browser-based) ---
    has_key = bool(cfg.get("api_key"))
    if has_key:
        try:
            with OctopusClient(base_url=base_url, api_key=cfg["api_key"]) as c:
                user = c.whoami()
            console.print(f"  [green]✓[/green] Already authenticated as [bold]{user['name']}[/bold]")
        except OctopusError:
            has_key = False

    if not has_key:
        import time as _time
        import webbrowser

        import httpx

        # Create a CLI auth session
        try:
            resp = httpx.post(f"{base_url}/api/v1/users/cli-auth/sessions", timeout=10)
            resp.raise_for_status()
            session_id = resp.json()["session_id"]
        except Exception as e:
            console.print(f"[red]Could not reach {base_url}: {e}[/red]")
            raise typer.Exit(1)

        # Build the login URL — for self-hosted, frontend is on port 3000
        if "localhost" in base_url or "127.0.0.1" in base_url:
            frontend_url = base_url.replace(":3456", ":3000")
        else:
            frontend_url = base_url
        login_url = f"{frontend_url}/login?cli={session_id}"

        console.print(f"\n  Opening browser to sign in...")
        console.print(f"  [dim]{login_url}[/dim]\n")
        webbrowser.open(login_url)

        _reserve_bottom_padding(4)
        console.print("  Waiting for authentication... [dim](press Ctrl+C to cancel)[/dim]")

        # Poll for the result
        try:
            for _ in range(120):  # 2 minutes max
                _time.sleep(1)
                try:
                    poll = httpx.get(f"{base_url}/api/v1/users/cli-auth/sessions/{session_id}", timeout=5)
                    if poll.status_code == 200:
                        result = poll.json()
                        if result["status"] == "complete":
                            save_config(api_key=result["api_key"], username=result["username"])
                            console.print(f"  [green]✓[/green] Logged in as [bold]{result['username']}[/bold]")
                            break
                except httpx.HTTPError:
                    pass
            else:
                console.print("[red]Timed out waiting for authentication.[/red]")
                raise typer.Exit(1)
        except KeyboardInterrupt:
            console.print("\n[yellow]Cancelled.[/yellow]")
            raise typer.Exit(1)

    # Reload config after auth
    cfg = load_config()

    with OctopusClient(base_url=base_url, api_key=cfg["api_key"]) as c:
        # --- Step 3: Workspace ---
        try:
            my_workspaces = c.list_workspaces(mine=True)
        except OctopusError:
            my_workspaces = []

        workspace_id = cfg.get("default_workspace", "")
        if my_workspaces:

content.text:
No — it doesn't fully do what you want yet. The auth step is now browser-based, but the rest of the flow still has friction:

1. **Scope** (user vs project) — still requires a choice before anything happens
2. **Managed vs self-host** — still requires a choice
3. **Workspace** — still asks you to type/paste a UUID or name

If the goal is "press Enter to authenticate," the flow should be more like:

1. Open browser immediately (default to managed)
2. Wait for auth
3. Auto-select the user's workspace (registration already creates one)
4. Done

Want me to streamline it to that — default to managed/user-scope, open the browser right away, and auto-pick the workspace if there's only one?

content.content:
version: "3.9"

# Octopus — production / self-hosting deployment
#
# Usage:
#   cp .env.example .env        # fill in required values (see README)
#   docker compose -f docker-compose.prod.yml up -d
#
# Required env vars in .env:
#   POSTGRES_USER, POSTGRES_PASSWORD, POSTGRES_DB
#   ANTHROPIC_API_KEY           (curation + universal search)
#   OPENAI_API_KEY              (semantic embeddings)
#   PUBLIC_URL                  (your public frontend URL, e.g. https://app.example.com)
#   CORS_ORIGINS                (comma-separated allowed origins)

services:
  postgres:
    image: pgvector/pgvector:pg16
    restart: always
    expose:
      - "5432"        # not published to host — only reachable by other services
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}
      POSTGRES_DB: ${POSTGRES_DB}
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${POSTGRES_USER}"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  backend:
    image: ghcr.io/Fergana-Labs/octopus-backend:latest
    restart: always
    expose:
      - "3456"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    env_file: .env
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://${POSTGRES_USER}:${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}@postgres:5432/${POSTGRES_DB}
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:3456/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3

  frontend:
    image: ghcr.io/Fergana-Labs/octopus-frontend:latest
    restart: always
    expose:
      - "3457"
    depends_on:
      - backend
    environment:
      NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL: ${PUBLIC_URL}

  caddy:
    image: caddy:2-alpine
    restart: always
    ports:
      - "80:80"
      - "443:443"
    volumes:
      - ./Caddyfile:/etc/caddy/Caddyfile:ro
      - caddy_data:/data
      - caddy_config:/config
    depends_on:
      - backend
      - frontend

volumes:
  postgres_data:
  caddy_data:
  caddy_config:

content.content:
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# Octopus — environment variable reference
# Copy to .env and fill in values for your environment.
# Variables marked [required] must be set for that feature to work.
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────

# ── Server ───────────────────────────────────
PORT=3456

# ── Database ─────────────────────────────────
# Used by docker-compose.prod.yml. Choose strong credentials.
POSTGRES_USER=octopus
POSTGRES_PASSWORD=changeme
POSTGRES_DB=octopus

# Full connection string used by the backend directly.
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://octopus:changeme@postgres:5432/octopus

# Connection pool sizing. Increase DB_POOL_MAX for high-traffic deployments.
DB_POOL_MIN=2
DB_POOL_MAX=20

# ── URLs & CORS ───────────────────────────────
# PUBLIC_URL is the frontend origin (used for invite links, CORS defaults).
# ⚠ PRODUCTION: set to your actual domain, e.g. https://app.yourcompany.com
PUBLIC_URL=http://localhost:3457
CORS_ORIGINS=http://localhost:3457,http://localhost:3456

# ── Embeddings ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Provider: openai | huggingface | local | auto (default).
# "auto" detects from available keys: OPENAI_API_KEY → openai, HF_TOKEN → huggingface, else → local.
# EMBEDDING_PROVIDER=auto

# Option 1: OpenAI (or any OpenAI-compatible API — Gemini, Cohere, Nomic, etc.)
OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-proj-...your-key-here...
# EMBEDDING_API_KEY=sk-...                  # optional override for embedding-only key
# EMBEDDING_API_URL=https://api.openai.com/v1/embeddings
# EMBEDDING_MODEL=text-embedding-3-small

# Option 2: Hugging Face Inference API (any HF Hub model)
# HF_TOKEN=hf_...
# EMBEDDING_MODEL=BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5          # default

# Option 3: Local sentence-transformers (free, no API key, downloads model on first use)
# EMBEDDING_PROVIDER=local
# EMBEDDING_MODEL=all-MiniLM-L6-v2          # default

# Shared
# EMBEDDING_DIMS=384                        # vector dimensions (must match your DB schema)

# ── File Storage (S3-compatible) ─────────────
# Octopus supports any S3-compatible object store (AWS S3, Cloudflare R2, MinIO).
# Leave blank to disable file uploads.
# S3_ENDPOINT=https://<account>.r2.cloudflarestorage.com
# S3_BUCKET=octopus
# S3_ACCESS_KEY=...
# S3_SECRET_KEY=...
# S3_REGION=auto

Okay, here's what I found:

**Current hosting:**
- **Frontend** (`stash-web` Vercel project): Deployed to `www.stash.ac`, root directory is `frontend/` in this repo
- **Backend**: Not on Vercel — the prod setup uses Docker (Caddy + Postgres + FastAPI). No evidence of a live backend deployment yet.
- `stash.ac` currently serves a different product ("Stash - Edit Slides With AI" with Auth0). So either that's being replaced or it's from a different branch.

**The rename from octopus to stash** touches a lot — CLI command name, config paths (`~/.octopus/`), package name, Docker images, API prefix `mc_`, every user-facing string, `getoctopus.com` references, etc. 

Before I dive into a massive rename, two questions:

1. **Backend hosting** — where is/will the backend run? The frontend on Vercel can't run FastAPI. Is there a separate server (Fly.io, Railway, a VPS) for the backend, or are you planning to deploy it somewhere? This matters because the CLI needs to know the API URL.

2. **Rename scope** — do you want the full rename now (CLI binary name `stash`, config dir `~/.stash/`, all strings, package names) or just update the user-facing references (the `connect` flow URL, branding strings) to point at `stash.ac`?

input.command:
gh pr create --title "Browser-based auth for CLI onboarding" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Replaces the CLI's username/password prompts during `octopus connect` with a browser-based auth flow (like `gh auth login`)
- CLI creates a session, opens the browser to `/login?cli=<session_id>`, and polls until the user authenticates
- Adds CLI auth session endpoints to the backend (`POST/GET /api/v1/users/cli-auth/sessions`)
- Updates the frontend login page with sign-in/sign-up toggle and CLI session approval
- If the user is already logged in on the web, opening the CLI link auto-approves immediately

## Files changed
- `backend/routers/users.py` — CLI auth session endpoints (in-memory, 10min TTL)
- `cli/main.py` — `connect` command uses `webbrowser.open` + polling instead of terminal prompts
- `frontend/src/app/login/page.tsx` — handles `?cli=` param, sign-in/sign-up toggle, approval flow

## Test plan
- [ ] Run `octopus connect`, verify browser opens to login page
- [ ] Sign in on the web, verify CLI picks up the API key and prints success
- [ ] Test with an already-logged-in browser session (should auto-approve)
- [ ] Test the register flow from the login page toggle
- [ ] Verify Ctrl+C cancels the polling cleanly
- [ ] Verify timeout after 2 minutes if no auth

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" --base main 2>&1

toolUseResult.prompt:
In /Users/henrydowling/projects/octopus, I need a comprehensive audit of every place the name "octopus" appears that would need to change in a rename to "stash". Be very thorough. Look for:

1. File/directory names containing "octopus" (case-insensitive)
2. Code references: imports, module names, package names, class names
3. Config: pyproject.toml, package.json, docker-compose files, Dockerfiles, Caddyfile, .env files
4. User-facing strings: CLI help text, console output, error messages
5. Config paths: ~/.octopus/, .octopus/, config file references
6. URLs: getoctopus.com, any domain references
7. Docker image names (ghcr.io/Fergana-Labs/octopus-*)
8. API key prefix (mc_ — does it stand for something octopus-related?)
9. GitHub references (Fergana-Labs/octopus)
10. CLI command name ("octopus" binary)
11. Comments and docstrings mentioning octopus
12. Frontend: page titles, meta tags, component names
13. Plugin paths referencing octopus

Group results by category. For each match, give the file path and a brief note on what needs to change. Be exhaustive — missing one reference means a broken rename.

1	import secrets
2	import time
3	
4	from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, Query, Request, status
5	
6	from ..auth import get_current_user
7	from ..middleware import limiter
8	from ..models import (
9	    LoginRequest,
10	    UserProfile,
11	    UserRegisterRequest,
12	    UserRegisterResponse,
13	    UserSearchResult,
14	    UserUpdateRequest,
15	)
16	from ..services import user_service
17	
18	router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/v1/users", tags=["users"])
19	
20	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
21	# CLI auth sessions — in-memory, short-lived (10 min TTL)
22	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
23	
24	_CLI_AUTH_TTL = 600  # seconds
25	_cli_sessions: dict[str, dict] = {}  # session_id → {created_at, api_key?, username?}
26	
27	
28	def _prune_cli_sessions() -> None:
29	    cutoff = time.time() - _CLI_AUTH_TTL
30	    expired = [k for k, v in _cli_sessions.items() if v["created_at"] < cutoff]
31	    for k in expired:
32	        del _cli_sessions[k]
33	
34	
35	@router.post("/register", response_model=UserRegisterResponse, status_code=201)
36	@limiter.limit("5/minute")
37	async def register(request: Request, req: UserRegisterRequest):
38	    try:
39	        user, api_key = await user_service.register_user(
40	            name=req.name,
41	            display_name=req.display_name,
42	            description=req.description,
43	            password=req.password,
44	        )
45	    except ValueError as e:
46	        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_409_CONFLICT, detail=str(e))
47	    return UserRegisterResponse(
48	        id=user["id"],
49	        name=user["name"],
50	        display_name=user["display_name"],
51	        api_key=api_key,
52	    )
53	
54	
55	@router.post("/login", response_model=UserRegisterResponse)
56	@limiter.limit("10/minute")
57	async def login(request: Request, req: LoginRequest):
58	    try:
59	        user, api_key = await user_service.authenticate_by_password(
60	            name=req.name, password=req.password
61	        )
62	    except ValueError as e:
63	        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED, detail=str(e))
64	    return UserRegisterResponse(
65	        id=user["id"],
66	        name=user["name"],
67	        display_name=user["display_name"],
68	        api_key=api_key,
69	    )
70	
71	
72	@router.get("/me", response_model=UserProfile)
73	async def get_me(current_user: dict = Depends(get_current_user)):
74	    return UserProfile(**current_user)
75	
76	
77	@router.patch("/me", response_model=UserProfile)
78	async def update_me(req: UserUpdateRequest, current_user: dict = Depends(get_current_user)):
79	    updated = await user_service.update_user(
80	        user_id=current_user["id"],
81	        display_name=req.display_name,
82	        description=req.description,
83	        password=req.password,
84	    )
85	    return UserProfile(**updated)
86	
87	
88	@router.get("/search", response_model=list[UserSearchResult])
89	async def search_users(
90	    q: str = Query(..., min_length=1, max_length=64),
91	    current_user: dict = Depends(get_current_user),
92	):
93	    """Search for users by name or display name."""
94	    from ..database import get_pool
95	
96	    pool = get_pool()
97	    rows = await pool.fetch(
98	        "SELECT id, name, display_name FROM users "
99	        "WHERE (name ILIKE $1 OR display_name ILIKE $1) AND id != $2 "
100	        "LIMIT 20",
101	        f"%{q}%",
102	        current_user["id"],
103	    )
104	    return [UserSearchResult(**dict(r)) for r in rows]
105	
106	
107	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
108	# CLI browser-based auth flow
109	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
110	
111	@router.post("/cli-auth/sessions")
112	@limiter.limit("10/minute")
113	async def create_cli_auth_session(request: Request):
114	    """Create a CLI auth session. Returns a session_id the CLI uses to poll."""
115	    _prune_cli_sessions()
116	    session_id = secrets.token_urlsafe(32)
117	    _cli_sessions[session_id] = {"created_at": time.time()}
118	    return {"session_id": session_id}
119	
120	
121	@router.get("/cli-auth/sessions/{session_id}")
122	@limiter.limit("60/minute")
123	async def poll_cli_auth_session(request: Request, session_id: str):
124	    """Poll for CLI auth result. Returns pending or complete with api_key."""
125	    _prune_cli_sessions()
126	    session = _cli_sessions.get(session_id)
127	    if not session:
128	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Session not found or expired")
129	    if "api_key" in session:
130	        del _cli_sessions[session_id]
131	        return {"status": "complete", "api_key": session["api_key"], "username": session["username"]}
132	    return {"status": "pending"}
133	
134	
135	@router.post("/cli-auth/sessions/{session_id}/approve")
136	@limiter.limit("10/minute")
137	async def approve_cli_auth_session(request: Request, session_id: str):
138	    """Called by the frontend after login to approve the CLI session."""
139	    body = await request.json()
140	    api_key = body.get("api_key")
141	    username = body.get("username")
142	    if not api_key or not username:
143	        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="api_key and username required")
144	    _prune_cli_sessions()
145	    session = _cli_sessions.get(session_id)
146	    if not session:
147	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Session not found or expired")
148	    session["api_key"] = api_key
149	    session["username"] = username
150	    return {"status": "approved"}

input.prompt:
I need to plan a comprehensive rename of a product from "Octopus" to "Stash". The codebase is at /Users/henrydowling/projects/octopus. Here's the full context:

## Product
- A shared memory/wiki/tables platform for AI agents, with a Python backend (FastAPI), Next.js frontend, and a Python CLI
- Hosted: frontend on Vercel (project `stash-web` at `www.stash.ac`), backend on Render
- Zero production users, so no migration/backward-compat needed
- The GitHub repo is `Fergana-Labs/octopus`

## What needs renaming (from exhaustive audit)

### User-facing / high-priority
1. **CLI binary**: `octopus` → `stash` (pyproject.toml entry point)
2. **Config dirs**: `~/.octopus/` → `~/.stash/`, `.octopus/` → `.stash/`
3. **URLs**: `getoctopus.com` → `stash.ac` throughout
4. **Docker images**: `ghcr.io/Fergana-Labs/octopus-backend` / `octopus-frontend`
5. **Environment variables**: `OCTOPUS_URL`, `OCTOPUS_API_KEY`, `OCTOPUS_AUTO_CURATE`, `OCTOPUS_SKIP_AUTO_CURATE`, `OCTOPUS_CURSOR_DATA`, `OCTOPUS_GEMINI_DATA`, `OCTOPUS_CODEX_DATA`, `OCTOPUS_OPENCODE_DATA`, `OCTOPUS_PYTHON` — all `OCTOPUS_*` → `STASH_*`
6. **Frontend**: page titles ("Octopus" → "Stash"), hero text, meta tags
7. **Plugin names**: `octopus` plugin → `stash` plugin in plugin.json, marketplace.json
8. **Plugin slash commands**: `/octopus:connect`, `/octopus:search`, etc. → `/stash:*`

### Code-level
9. **Classes**: `OctopusClient` → `StashClient`, `OctopusError` → `StashError` (in cli/client.py AND plugins/shared/octopus_client.py)
10. **File rename**: `plugins/shared/octopus_client.py` → `stash_client.py` + update all imports across plugins
11. **Package names**: pyproject.toml `name = "octopus"` → `"stash"`, frontend package.json `"name": "octopus"` → `"stash"`
12. **Logger**: `logging.getLogger("octopus")` → `"stash"`
13. **Plugin data dirs**: `PLUGIN_DATA_DIRS` dict in cli/main.py references `.claude/plugins/data/octopus` and `.octopus/plugins/*`
14. **LocalStorage key**: `octopus_selected_workspace` in AppSidebar.tsx, `octopus_token` in api.ts

### Database / infra
15. **DB defaults**: `POSTGRES_USER=octopus`, `POSTGRES_DB=octopus` in .env.example, docker-compose files, backend/config.py defaults, test configs
16. **Dockerfile**: Linux user/group `octopus` → `stash`
17. **Caddyfile**: comment header
18. **CI**: `.github/workflows/test.yml` DB setup commands

### Docs
19. **README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, USE_CASES.md, TESTING.md**: all references
20. **Plugin READMEs**: all 5 plugins have their own READMEs
21. **Frontend docs pages**: `frontend/src/app/docs/*.tsx` — multiple pages
22. **CLAUDE.md files**: plugin CLAUDE.md references

### API key prefix
23. **`mc_` prefix** in backend/auth.py — currently "mc_" + token. Decision: keep as-is (it's opaque to users and doesn't say "octopus") or change to `st_` for stash.

## Constraints
- Zero users, no backward compat needed
- The GitHub repo name itself (`Fergana-Labs/octopus`) — we probably won't rename the repo right now, just update references where practical
- The `stash.ac` domain is already configured in Vercel
- Backend is on Render

## Design the plan
Please design a phased implementation plan. Think about:
1. What order to do things to avoid breaking the build at each step
2. Whether to do it as one big PR or multiple
3. How to handle the file rename (octopus_client.py → stash_client.py) without breaking imports
4. Whether to rename the mc_ prefix or leave it
5. A verification strategy

Keep the plan concrete — list specific files and what changes in each.

content.text:
I now have a thorough understanding of every file and reference. Let me compile the plan.

---

Here is the comprehensive phased implementation plan for renaming Octopus to Stash.

## Decision: `mc_` prefix

**Recommendation: Leave `mc_` as-is.** It is opaque to users, checked in exactly one place (`backend/auth.py` lines 47 and 72-73), and changing it would invalidate any existing API keys in dev/staging databases. If you want to change it later to `st_`, it is a two-line change plus a migration, but there is no user-facing benefit right now.

## Strategy: One Large PR, Five Atomic Commits

With zero users, there is no value in shipping incrementally. A single feature branch with logically grouped commits is the cleanest approach. Each commit should leave tests passing.

## Verification Strategy

After all changes, run in this order:

1. **Grep audit**: `rg -i "octopus" --glob '!.git/**' --glob '!node_modules/**' --glob '!*.egg-info/**' --glob '!package-lock.json'` -- should return zero results (except possibly the git clone URL for the repo itself, which we chose to keep).

2. **Python tests**: `cd /Users/henrydowling/projects/octopus && python -m pytest -v` -- backend tests pass. Requires updating the local Postgres user/db or setting `TEST_DATABASE_URL` to match new names.

3. **Frontend tests**: `cd frontend && npm test` -- api.test.ts with updated localStorage keys.

4. **Lint**: `ruff check backend/ cli/` and `npm run lint` in frontend.

5. **CLI smoke test**: `pip install -e .` then `stash --help` (confirms entry point works).

6. **Docker build**: `docker compose build` (confirms Dockerfile user rename).

7. **Plugin import check**: `cd plugins/shared && python -c "from stash_client import StashClient; print('OK')"` -- confirms file rename + class rename.

---

## Key Risk: The File Rename

The file rename `plugins/shared/octopus_client.py` --> `plugins/shared/stash_client.py` is safe because:
- All imports are via `sys.path` manipulation (each plugin config.py adds `shared/` to `sys.path` then does `from octopus_client import OctopusClient`)
- Changing these to `from stash_client import StashClient` in the same commit as the file rename is atomic in git
- No external consumers (zero users)
- The `__pycache__` directories with the old `.pyc` files should be cleaned (`find . -name __pycache__ -exec rm -rf {} +`)
